metadata.description: Exact-sense Traditional Chinese corrections reviewed by Codex. Each row is bound to the existing English definition and records the source value so upstream changes cannot be applied silently.
metadata.policy: Correct only clear semantic or part-of-speech mismatches. Chinese definitions are independently reviewed, not generated by blindly translating the English gloss.
metadata.notes: Manual AI-assisted semantic corrections. Chinese definitions are independently reviewed, not generated by translating the English definition. 2026-06-27 batches prioritize high-confidence review candidates, reject noisy automatic suggestions, and keep natural Traditional Chinese glosses.

corrections.english: (orthography) A mark or character used in writing, in order to indicate the place of the spoken accent, or to indicate the nature or quality of the vowel marked.
corrections.reason: The source incorrectly narrows the general orthographic sense to an acute accent.

example.english: The accent above the letter changes its pronunciation.
example.chinese: 字母上方的重音符號會改變它的讀音。

corrections.english: (linguistics, sociolinguistics) The distinctive manner of pronouncing a language associated with a particular region, social group, etc., whether of a native speaker or a foreign speaker; the phonetic and phonological aspects of a dialect.
corrections.reason: This sense describes pronunciation, not a written accent mark.

example.english: She speaks English with a slight Scottish accent.
example.chinese: 她說英語時帶有輕微的蘇格蘭口音。

corrections.english: (prosody, poetry) Stress laid on certain syllables of a verse.
corrections.reason: The prosodic sense is stress placed on a syllable.

example.english: The accent falls on the first syllable of the line.
example.chinese: 這一行詩的重音落在第一個音節。

corrections.english: A rebuke by an authority that one has erred and should not persist in their actions; a reprimand.
corrections.reason: The definition does not require the rebuke to be mild.

example.english: He ignored the teacher's admonition and repeated the mistake.
example.chinese: 他無視老師的警告，再次犯下同樣的錯誤。

corrections.english: (transitive) To pronounce free from or give absolution for a penalty, blame, or guilt. [First attested in the mid 16th century.]
corrections.reason: This sense concerns release from guilt or blame, not completing something.

example.english: The court absolved her of all responsibility.
example.chinese: 法庭宣告她無須承擔任何責任，等同宣告無罪。

corrections.english: (transitive) To make shorter; to shorten (in time); to abridge; to shorten by ending sooner than planned. [First attested from around (1350 to 1470).]
corrections.reason: The source gives a noun-like abbreviated name instead of the verb.

example.english: The editor abbreviated the lengthy title.
example.chinese: 編輯把冗長的標題縮短了。

corrections.english: Leading away; a carrying away. [from early 17th century.]
corrections.reason: The source wording is nonstandard and unclear.

example.english: The story describes the abduction of a prince.
example.chinese: 故事描述了一位王子遭綁架的經過。

corrections.english: (legal) The wrongful, and usually forcible, carrying off of a human being. [from mid-18th century.]
corrections.reason: Abduction is carrying a person away, while extortion is a different offense.

example.english: The police investigated the child's abduction.
example.chinese: 警方調查該名兒童遭綁架的案件。

corrections.english: (transitive) To execute fully; to fulfill; to complete successfully.
corrections.reason: Accomplish means completing or achieving something, not stating it.

example.english: The team accomplished its main objective.
example.chinese: 團隊達成了主要目標。

corrections.english: A convenience, a fitting, something satisfying a need.
corrections.reason: This sense denotes a convenience or fitting, not lodging.

example.english: The building includes accommodation for wheelchair users.
example.chinese: 這座建築設有供輪椅使用者使用的便利設施。

corrections.english: Synthetic fibers made from a polymer.
corrections.reason: Acrylic fibre is a fibre made from polymer, not the resin itself.

example.english: The sweater is made from acrylic fiber.
example.chinese: 這件毛衣由丙烯酸纖維製成。

corrections.english: A habit or practice that damages, jeopardizes or shortens one's life but when ceased causes trauma.
corrections.reason: The definition describes addiction and withdrawal, not poisoning.

example.english: He sought treatment for his gambling addiction.
example.chinese: 他因賭博成癮而尋求治療。

corrections.english: (legal) The person charged with an offense; the defendant in a criminal case.
corrections.reason: The source value means receipt and is unrelated to the legal sense.

example.english: The accused appeared before the judge.
example.chinese: 被告在法官面前出庭。

corrections.english: Sticky; tenacious, as glutinous substances.
corrections.reason: The noun sticker was incorrectly used for an adjective describing stickiness.

example.english: The tape has an adhesive surface.
example.chinese: 這膠帶有黏性的表面。

corrections.english: Apt or tending to adhere; clinging.
corrections.reason: The noun sticker was incorrectly used for an adjective.

example.english: The adhesive material clung firmly to the wall.
example.chinese: 這種易黏附的材料牢牢貼在牆上。

corrections.english: A person who is in adolescence; someone who has reached puberty but is not yet an adult.
corrections.reason: The sense includes adolescents of any gender.

example.english: The programme provides support for adolescents.
example.chinese: 這項計劃為青少年提供支援。

corrections.english: Fully grown.
corrections.reason: The source gives a noun, while this entry is adjectival.

example.english: The adult bird has dark blue feathers.
example.chinese: 這隻成年的鳥有深藍色羽毛。

corrections.english: To arrive or begin, especially at the first coming or appearance of something.
corrections.reason: The source value means use or purpose and is unrelated.

example.english: A new era advented with the discovery.
example.chinese: 這項發現標誌著新時代的到來。

corrections.new_chinese: 附屬機構；分支機構；成員
corrections.english: Someone or something, especially, a television station, that is associated with a larger, related organization, such as a television network; a member of a group of associated things.
corrections.reason: The source confused an affiliate with a daughter.

example.english: The local station is an affiliate of the national network.
example.chinese: 這家地方電視台是全國電視網的附屬機構。

corrections.english: That which happens after, that which follows, usually of strongly negative connotation in most contexts, implying a preceding catastrophe.
corrections.reason: Aftermath is a consequence following a damaging event, not an answer.

example.english: The town struggled in the aftermath of the flood.
example.chinese: 小鎮努力應付洪水造成的後果。

corrections.new_chinese: 完全錯誤的；毫無根據的
corrections.english: (idiomatic) Utterly incorrect; erroneous; uninformed.
corrections.reason: The source value means sickle and is unrelated to the idiom.

example.english: His theory about the accident was all wet.
example.chinese: 他對事故的說法是完全錯誤的。

corrections.english: (poker) A hand where at least one player bets all of his or her chips.
corrections.reason: The poker term means betting all chips, not a carpet.

example.english: His all in forced the other players to decide quickly.
example.chinese: 他的全押迫使其他玩家迅速作出決定。

corrections.english: (education, of a school) Providing instruction for female students only.
corrections.reason: The source gives daughter rather than an adjective describing a school.

example.english: She attended an all-girls school.
example.chinese: 她曾就讀一所全女生的學校。

corrections.english: (American spelling, medicine) A substance administered to reduce the perception of pain or to induce numbness for surgery and may render the recipient unconscious.
corrections.reason: The definition describes a substance, not the field of anesthesiology.

example.english: The doctor administered a local anesthetic.
example.chinese: 醫生施用了局部麻醉劑。

corrections.english: Capable of being seen, or easily seen; open to view; visible to the eye; within sight or view.
corrections.reason: The source gives the noun visibility instead of an adjective.

example.english: The damage was apparent from across the room.
example.chinese: 損壞從房間另一端也是可見的。

corrections.english: Clear or manifest to the understanding; plain; evident; obvious; known; palpable; indubitable.
corrections.reason: The source gives a state of understanding rather than evident or obvious.

example.english: There were apparent signs of damage.
example.chinese: 現場有明顯的損壞跡象。

corrections.english: A small, light, and usually savory first course in a meal.
corrections.reason: The source means entrance and is unrelated to a meal course.

example.english: We ordered soup as an appetizer.
example.chinese: 我們點了湯作為開胃菜。

corrections.english: A tree of the genus Malus; especially Malus domestica which is cultivated for its edible fruit; the apple tree. [from 15th c.]
corrections.reason: This exact sense is the tree rather than its fruit.

example.english: They planted an apple beside the farmhouse.
example.chinese: 他們在農舍旁種了一棵蘋果樹。

corrections.english: One who applies for something; one who makes a request; a petitioner.
corrections.reason: An applicant is specifically someone who applies; not every applicant is a candidate.

example.english: Each applicant must provide two references.
example.chinese: 每名申請人都必須提供兩名推薦人。

corrections.english: The act of appointing a person to hold an office or to have a position of trust
corrections.reason: This sense is an act of appointing, not destiny.

example.english: Her appointment as director was announced today.
example.chinese: 她獲委任為董事的消息今天公布。

corrections.english: Stipulation; agreement; the act of fixing by mutual agreement.
corrections.reason: An agreement or stipulation is not necessarily a compromise.

example.english: The matter was settled by appointment between the parties.
example.chinese: 雙方透過協議解決了此事。

corrections.english: (government) The assignment of a person by an official to perform a duty, such as a presidential appointment of a judge to a court.
corrections.reason: The government sense is an official appointment, and the existing example illustrated a scheduled meeting.

example.english: The senate confirmed her appointment as a judge.
example.chinese: 參議院確認了她出任法官的任命。

corrections.english: (usually, of a decision) Based on individual discretion or judgment; not based on any objective distinction, perhaps even made at random.
corrections.reason: The source value means free of charge and is unrelated.

example.english: The choice appeared completely arbitrary.
example.chinese: 這項選擇看來完全是任意的。

corrections.english: A place for storing earlier, and often historical, material. An archive usually contains documents (letters, records, newspapers, etc.) or other types of media kept for historical interest.
corrections.reason: The definition is the place or repository, not an individual document.

example.english: The letters are preserved in the university archive.
example.chinese: 這些信件保存在大學檔案館內。

corrections.english: (transitive) To place (something) into an archive.
corrections.reason: The source gives noun fragments instead of the verb.

example.english: Please archive the completed project files.
example.chinese: 請把已完成的專案檔案歸檔。

corrections.english: (organic chemistry) Any monocyclic or polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon.
corrections.reason: The chemistry term arene was confused with arena.

example.english: Benzene is the simplest arene.
example.chinese: 苯是最簡單的芳香烴。

corrections.english: (transitive) To put together.
corrections.reason: The general sense is putting components together, not binding a book.

example.english: The workers assembled the machine in two hours.
example.chinese: 工人在兩小時內組裝好機器。

corrections.english: A tree of the genus Fraxinus, with compound leaves and a tough, flexible wood.
corrections.reason: Fraxinus is an ash tree, not a eucalyptus tree.

example.english: An old ash tree stood beside the gate.
example.chinese: 大門旁立著一棵古老的梣樹。

corrections.english: (computing) The symbol @, used in e-mail addresses to separate the user name from the domain name.
corrections.reason: The source value means ram and is unrelated to the symbol.

example.english: Every email address contains an at sign.
example.chinese: 每個電郵地址都包含一個@符號。

corrections.english: (transitive) To beat in a contest.
corrections.reason: The source confused beat or defeat with crash.

example.english: She bested the champion in the final.
example.chinese: 她在決賽中打敗了冠軍。

corrections.english: The outer edge of something.
corrections.reason: The source confused the edge sense with the South African currency rand.

example.english: A narrow path runs along the border of the field.
example.chinese: 一條窄路沿著田地的邊緣伸展。

corrections.english: A decorative strip around the edge of something.
corrections.reason: The source confused a decorative border with the currency rand.

example.english: The cloth has a gold border.
example.chinese: 這塊布有一道金色飾邊。

corrections.english: The contents of such a container.
corrections.reason: This sense denotes the contents or quantity, not the container.

example.english: They shared a bottle of wine.
example.chinese: 他們分享了一瓶的量的葡萄酒。

corrections.english: (figurative) Intoxicating liquor; alcohol.
corrections.reason: The figurative sense denotes alcohol, and the existing example illustrated the container.

example.english: He turned to the bottle after losing his job.
example.chinese: 他失業後開始借酒消愁，依賴酒精飲品。

corrections.english: (topology) (of a set) the set of points in the closure of a set S, not belonging to the interior of that set
corrections.reason: This is a mathematical boundary, not the currency rand.

example.english: The boundary contains the limiting points of the set.
example.chinese: 這個邊界（拓撲學）包含該集合的極限點。

corrections.english: A dessert made from ice cream frozen in a (generally spherical or hemispherical) mold.
corrections.reason: The dessert bombe was confused with an aerosol spray.

example.english: The restaurant served a chocolate bombe for dessert.
example.chinese: 餐廳以巧克力圓頂冰淇淋甜點作為餐後甜品。

corrections.english: (computing) An electromechanical device used in early cryptanalysis.
corrections.reason: The historical cryptanalytic machine was confused with an aerosol spray.

example.english: The bombe helped cryptanalysts test possible settings.
example.chinese: 炸彈機協助密碼分析員測試可能的設定。

corrections.english: (historical) Any grenade, mortar shell, or artillery shell (hollow metal object) containing a charge that can explode.
corrections.reason: The historical weapon sense was confused with an aerosol spray.

example.english: The museum displayed an old artillery bombshell.
example.chinese: 博物館展出了一枚舊式炮彈。

corrections.english: (figurative) Something that is very surprising, shocking, amazing or sensational.
corrections.reason: The figurative sense is shocking news or information.

example.english: The resignation announcement was a political bombshell.
example.chinese: 辭職公告是一則政治上的爆炸性消息。

corrections.new_chinese: 性感尤物；非常迷人的人
corrections.english: (by extension) Someone who is very attractive; a sex symbol.
corrections.reason: The person sense was confused with an aerosol spray.

example.english: The actor was promoted as a Hollywood bombshell.
example.chinese: 這名演員被宣傳為荷里活性感尤物。

corrections.english: The topmost lip or rim of a container, or a natural feature shaped like a container.
corrections.reason: The source confused a rim with the currency rand.

example.english: Fill the glass almost to the brim.
example.chinese: 把杯子加至接近口沿的位置。

corrections.english: The edge, margin, or border of a steep place, as of a precipice; a bank or edge.
corrections.reason: The source confused an edge with the currency rand.

example.english: They stopped at the brink of the cliff.
example.chinese: 他們在懸崖邊停下來。

corrections.english: (figurative) The edge or border.
corrections.reason: The figurative sense is a threshold, not the currency rand.

example.english: The company was on the brink of collapse.
example.chinese: 公司正處於倒閉的邊緣。

corrections.english: (anatomy) The chest, or front of the human thorax.
corrections.reason: This sense is the chest generally, not specifically a mammary organ.

example.english: He folded his arms across his breast.
example.chinese: 他把雙臂交叉放在胸部前。

corrections.english: (soccer) A midfield player who specialises more in attacking duties.
corrections.reason: The source value means ankle sock and is unrelated to football.

example.english: The attacking midfielder created two goals.
example.chinese: 那名進攻中場策動了兩個入球。

corrections.english: (chiefly, US) Lead shot used in shotgun cartridges, made of larger pellets than birdshot and suitable for hunting larger game. [from Template:SAFESUBST: c.]
corrections.reason: Buckshot is shotgun ammunition, not hail.

example.english: The shell was loaded with buckshot.
example.chinese: 這枚霰彈殼裝有大型霰彈。

corrections.english: A time-sensitive news item or short news report.
corrections.reason: A bulletin is a short news report, not a musical note.

example.english: The station interrupted the programme with an emergency bulletin.
example.chinese: 電台中斷節目，播出一則緊急公告。

corrections.english: Of food or drink, having the rich taste or thick, smooth texture of cream, whether or not it actually contains cream.
corrections.reason: The source translation is an unrelated musical term.

example.english: Blend the soup until it becomes creamy.
example.chinese: 把湯攪拌至奶油般濃郁順滑的質感。

corrections.english: Having the colour of cream.
corrections.reason: The source translation is an unrelated musical term.

example.english: The walls were painted a creamy white.
example.chinese: 牆壁被漆成奶油色的白色。

corrections.english: Straightforward, open and sincere.
corrections.reason: The source gives an abstract noun-like expression instead of an adjective.

example.english: She gave a candid answer.
example.chinese: 她給了一個坦率的回答。

corrections.english: The metacarpal of a horse.
corrections.reason: The horse bone was confused with the musical canon.

example.english: The horse injured the cannon bone in its front leg.
example.chinese: 這匹馬前腿的管骨（馬的掌骨）受了傷。

corrections.english: The metatarsal of a horse.
corrections.reason: The horse bone was confused with the musical canon.

example.english: The scan showed a fracture of the hind cannon bone.
example.chinese: 掃描顯示後腿的管骨（馬的蹠骨）骨折。

corrections.english: A group of literary works that are generally accepted as representing a field.
corrections.reason: The literary canon was confused with the musical form.

example.english: The novel is now part of the modern literary canon.
example.chinese: 這部小說現已成為現代文學經典作品的一部分。

corrections.english: A religious law or body of law decreed by the church.
corrections.reason: The religious law sense was confused with the musical form.

example.english: The decision was made according to church canon.
example.chinese: 這項決定依照教會法規作出。

corrections.english: A piece of music in which the same melody is played by different voices, but beginning at different times; a round.
corrections.reason: The musical meaning is correct and is normalized to Traditional Chinese.

example.english: The choir performed the melody as a canon.
example.chinese: 合唱團以卡農形式演唱這段旋律。

corrections.english: A chubby, plump person.
corrections.reason: The source gives an adjective-like quality instead of a person noun.

example.english: The character is portrayed as a cheerful chubby.
example.chinese: 這個角色被描繪成一個開朗而胖乎乎的人。

corrections.english: (transitive, computing, slang) To overwrite (data) or override (an assignment of a value), often unintentionally or unexpectedly.
corrections.reason: The computing slang means overwriting data, not crashing.

example.english: The update clobbered the existing configuration.
example.chinese: 這次更新覆寫了現有設定。

corrections.english: (transitive) To restore to health.
corrections.reason: This definition means restoring health, which is stronger than merely treating.

example.english: The medicine cured the infection.
example.chinese: 這種藥治癒了感染。

corrections.english: An ardent enthusiast or admirer.
corrections.reason: The source gives an adjective-like abstract quality instead of a person.

example.english: The exhibition attracted devotees of modern art.
example.chinese: 這個展覽吸引了許多現代藝術愛好者。

corrections.english: Dishonest.
corrections.reason: The source translation is an unrelated musical term.

example.english: He was involved in several dodgy business deals.
example.chinese: 他涉及數宗不誠實的商業交易。

corrections.english: (slang) A sexually attractive person.
corrections.reason: The slang person sense was confused with the musical canon.

example.english: The magazine described the actor as a real dish.
example.chinese: 雜誌形容這名演員是一個性感迷人的人。

corrections.new_chinese: 夢中情人；非常迷人的人
corrections.english: (informal, dated, or, humorous) An exceptionally good-looking and sexually attractive person, particularly a man.
corrections.reason: The attractive person sense was confused with the musical canon.

example.english: She jokingly called the handsome actor her dreamboat.
example.chinese: 她開玩笑地稱那名英俊演員為夢中情人。

corrections.english: (transitive) To make appear (much) smaller, puny, tiny; to be much larger than.
corrections.reason: The verb describes making something look small by comparison.

example.english: The new tower dwarfs every nearby building.
example.chinese: 新塔使附近所有建築物都顯得矮小。

corrections.new_chinese: 相形見絀；顯得微不足道
corrections.english: (transitive) To make appear insignificant.
corrections.reason: This figurative sense means making something seem insignificant.

example.english: The scale of the disaster dwarfed earlier incidents.
example.chinese: 這場災難的規模使早前的事件相形見絀。

corrections.english: To hinder from growing to the natural size; to make or keep small; to stunt.
corrections.reason: This biological sense means stunting growth.

example.english: Poor soil can dwarf the plants.
example.chinese: 貧瘠的土壤會阻礙生長，使植物保持矮小。

corrections.english: An annotation placed at the end of a document or chapter of a document.
corrections.reason: Endnote means a note at the end of a text, not a musical note.

example.english: The source is identified in an endnote at the end of the chapter.
example.chinese: 資料來源標示在本章末尾的一則尾註中。

corrections.new_chinese: 充滿（負面事物）的；伴隨著…的
corrections.english: Followed by with: carrying, or charged or loaded up with (usually something negative); accompanied by; entailing.
corrections.reason: The source translation is unrelated to the adjective.

example.english: The negotiations were fraught with difficulty.
example.chinese: 談判充滿困難。

corrections.new_chinese: 令人焦慮的；令人憂慮的
corrections.english: (specifically) Carrying or loaded with anxiety, fear, or stress, for example, due to complexity or difficulty; distressed; also, causing distress; distressing.
corrections.reason: The source translation means de facto and does not match anxiety or distress.

example.english: It was a fraught journey through the storm.
example.chinese: 那是一段令人焦慮的暴風雨旅程。

corrections.english: Of or relating to the natives or inhabitants of Germany; to people of German descent; to their cultures.
corrections.reason: This adjective concerns Germany, its people, or culture, not specifically the language; the existing example illustrated the language sense.

example.english: The festival celebrates German culture.
example.chinese: 這個節慶活動弘揚德國的文化。

corrections.english: A number of townships in the United States, listed under German Township.
corrections.reason: This proper-noun sense is a group of US place names, not the German language; the existing example illustrated the language.

example.english: Several states have a township named German.
example.chinese: 美國多個州都有名為日耳曼鎮區的地方。

corrections.english: Over (a period of time).
corrections.reason: The source means previously and does not express duration.

example.english: They stayed there for three weeks.
example.chinese: 他們在那裡逗留達三個星期。

corrections.english: Known to one, or generally known; commonplace.
corrections.reason: The noun sense familiar spirit was incorrectly used for the adjective.

example.english: The melody sounded familiar.
example.chinese: 這段旋律聽起來很熟悉的。

corrections.english: Intimate or friendly.
corrections.reason: The noun sense familiar spirit was incorrectly used for the adjective.

example.english: They greeted each other in a familiar manner.
example.chinese: 他們以親密的方式互相問候。

corrections.english: Acquainted.
corrections.reason: The source gives a person noun instead of an adjective.

example.english: Are you familiar with this software?
example.chinese: 這是你熟悉的軟體嗎？

corrections.english: A colored or gilded border.
corrections.reason: The decorative border sense was confused with the currency rand.

example.english: A narrow gold fillet framed the panel.
example.chinese: 一道狹窄的金邊圍著面板。

corrections.english: (British, informal, chiefly, slang) Sexually attractive; good-looking; fanciable.
corrections.reason: The British slang adjective was confused with the musical canon.

example.english: His friends thought the new singer was fit.

example.english: The workers gathered apples from the orchard.
example.chinese: 工人在果園收穫蘋果。

corrections.english: Happening once every year.
corrections.reason: The adjective describes annual frequency, not a plant with a one-year life cycle.

example.english: The equipment receives a yearly inspection.
example.chinese: 這些設備接受一年一次的檢查。

corrections.english: (transitive) To cut down grass or crops.
corrections.reason: The verb means cutting grass or crops; the source value means magpie.

example.english: He mowed the grass before the rain.
example.chinese: 他在下雨前割了草。

corrections.english: An appraisal or evaluation.
corrections.reason: An assessment is the appraisal process or result, not necessarily a numerical score.

example.english: The doctor made a careful assessment of her condition.
example.chinese: 醫生仔細評估了她的病情。

corrections.english: A castrated goat.
corrections.reason: The animal term was confused with the homophone weather.

example.english: The farmer kept the wether with the rest of the flock.
example.chinese: 農夫把那隻閹羊和羊群養在一起。

corrections.english: (golf) A sand or water obstacle on a golf course.
corrections.reason: The golf sense is a sand or water obstacle, not an opportunity.

example.english: His ball landed in a water hazard.
example.chinese: 他的球落入了水障礙區。

corrections.english: (usually follows interrogative adverbs) Otherwise, if not.
corrections.reason: This adverbial sense means otherwise or if not, not different.

example.english: Hurry, else we will miss the train.
example.chinese: 快一點，否則我們會錯過火車。

corrections.english: The currency of Iceland, Denmark (including Greenland and the Faroe Islands) and Norway, divided into 100 øre, except in Iceland where 1 króna = 100 aurar.
corrections.reason: The currency name should be transliterated as krone, not translated as a ceremonial crown.

example.english: The ticket cost one hundred kroner.
example.chinese: 這張票售價一百克朗。

corrections.english: Goods which are prohibited from being traded, smuggled goods.
corrections.reason: The entry denotes prohibited goods, while the source gives the activity of smuggling.

example.english: Customs officers found contraband in the vehicle.
example.chinese: 海關人員在車內發現了違禁品。

corrections.english: (now, _, chiefly, _, literary) A girl or an unmarried young woman.
corrections.reason: The literary noun means a girl or unmarried young woman, not a female servant.

example.english: The old tale tells of a brave maiden.
example.chinese: 這個古老故事講述一位勇敢的少女。

corrections.english: (dated, outside, UK, and, Australia) A man, a fellow.
corrections.reason: The dated colloquial noun means a man or fellow, not a traveller.

example.english: He is a friendly old chap.
example.chinese: 他是個友善的老男人。

corrections.english: Drawing upon another organism for sustenance.
corrections.reason: The source gives a noun, while this adjective describes an organism living parasitically.

example.english: The plant is parasitic on nearby trees.
example.chinese: 這是一種寄生的植物，依附附近樹木生長。

corrections.english: The act of accomplishing; completion; fulfilment.
corrections.reason: The entry denotes the act of completion, while the source gives a verb.

example.english: The accomplishment of the plan took several years.
example.chinese: 這項計劃的完成花了數年時間。

corrections.english: Of a creature or object: growing, living, or existing in a woodland.
corrections.reason: The source gives a place noun instead of an adjective relating to woodland.

example.english: Woodland birds nested near the path.
example.chinese: 森林的鳥類在小徑附近築巢。

corrections.english: The manner, pose, or posture in which one stands.
corrections.reason: This sense is a standing posture, not a general location.

example.english: The athlete widened her stance.
example.chinese: 那名運動員把站姿調得更寬。

corrections.english: Pertaining to an economy.
corrections.reason: The source gives the noun economics instead of an adjective relating to an economy.

example.english: The country introduced an economic reform.
example.chinese: 該國推行了一項與經濟有關的改革。

corrections.english: An airy pudding served chilled, particularly chocolate mousse.
corrections.reason: The culinary sense is the chilled dessert mousse, not physical foam.

example.english: We shared a chocolate mousse.
example.chinese: 我們分享了一份朱古力慕斯。

corrections.english: (intransitive) To obtain a level of success or fame; to succeed.
corrections.reason: This figurative sense means achieving success or fame, not creating a work.

example.english: She felt that she had finally arrived as an artist.
example.chinese: 她覺得自己終於以藝術家身分成名。

corrections.english: To produce offspring sexually; to bear young.
corrections.reason: The biological sense means producing offspring, not manufacturing.

example.english: These birds breed in early spring.
example.chinese: 這些鳥在初春繁殖。

corrections.english: Of or relating to an age between thirteen and nineteen years old.
corrections.reason: The source gives a person noun instead of the adjective.

example.english: The programme supports teenage students.
example.chinese: 這項計劃支援青少年的學習需要。

corrections.english: In motion, in progress; being done or carried out; on a journey.
corrections.reason: The adverb means in progress or in motion, not development as a noun.

example.english: The investigation is already underway.
example.chinese: 調查已在進行中。

corrections.english: (transitive, medicine) To show or manifest by symptoms.
corrections.reason: The medical sense means showing or manifesting through symptoms.

example.english: The symptoms indicate a possible infection.
example.chinese: 這些症狀顯示可能有感染。

corrections.english: The luminous plasma atmosphere of the Sun (the solar corona) or other star, extending millions of kilometres into space, most easily seen during a total solar eclipse.
corrections.reason: The astronomical sense is a star's luminous corona, not a ceremonial crown.

example.english: The Sun's corona became visible during the eclipse.
example.chinese: 日食期間可以看見太陽的日冕。

corrections.english: (countable, automotive) A configuration of the transmission of a motor car so as to achieve a particular ratio of engine to axle torque.
corrections.reason: The automotive sense is a transmission gear, not race walking.

example.english: She shifted the car into a lower gear.
example.chinese: 她把汽車換到較低的檔位。

corrections.english: A club made of wood or aluminium used for striking the ball in sports such as baseball, softball and cricket.
corrections.reason: This sporting sense is the implement used to hit a ball, not the animal.

example.english: He swung the baseball bat.
example.chinese: 他揮動棒球球棒。

corrections.english: Sophisticated, especially because of surfeit; versed in the ways of the world.
corrections.reason: The adjective describes worldly experience, not the planet Earth.

example.english: She gave him some worldly advice.
example.chinese: 她給了他一些世故的忠告。

corrections.english: A small nocturnal insect pest of humans, of species Cimex lectularius (common bedbug) and Cimex hemipterus (tropical bedbug) that feeds on the blood of humans during sleep.
corrections.reason: The insect was confused with a drawing pin.

example.english: The hotel treated the room for bedbugs.
example.chinese: 酒店為房間進行床蝨滅蟲處理。

corrections.english: A leader of a group of people, especially an unofficial group.
corrections.reason: A ringleader is the leader of an unofficial or troublesome group, not an employer.

example.english: Police arrested the ringleader of the group.
example.chinese: 警方拘捕了該團伙的頭目。

corrections.english: (architectural element) The triangular area at the peak of an external wall adjacent to, and terminating, two sloped roof surfaces (pitches).
corrections.reason: The architectural feature was confused with a pine nut.

example.english: A small window was set into the gable.
example.chinese: 山牆上裝有一扇小窗。

corrections.english: A class of society composed of people with similar social, cultural, or economic status.
corrections.reason: This sociological sense denotes a social class, not a diaper.

example.english: The survey included people from every social stratum.
example.chinese: 這項調查涵蓋每個社會階層的人。

corrections.english: A post bearing a sign that gives information on directions.
corrections.reason: A signpost gives directions from a fixed post; it is not a vehicle indicator.

example.english: The signpost pointed toward the village.
example.chinese: 路標指向那條村莊。

corrections.english: A non-existent or empty value or set of values.
corrections.reason: The computing and mathematical noun denotes a null or empty value.

example.english: The database returned a null for that field.
example.chinese: 資料庫為該欄位傳回空值。

corrections.english: Tending to stick; able to adhere via the drying of a viscous substance.
corrections.reason: The adjective describes adhesion; the source value means tights.

example.english: The label has a sticky surface.
example.chinese: 這個標籤有黏性的表面。

corrections.english: (UK, offensive, ethnic slur) A German person, usually male.
corrections.reason: The offensive ethnic term denotes a German man, not sauerkraut.

example.english: The dated insult Fritz is offensive to German people.
example.chinese: 過時稱呼「德國佬」是針對德國人的冒犯性用語。

corrections.english: (amino acid) A nonessential amino acid, amino-acetic acid, C2H5NO2 found in most proteins but especially in sugar cane; the simplest amino acid.
corrections.reason: The biochemical sense is the amino acid glycine, not the plant genus Glycine or wisteria.

example.english: Glycine is the simplest amino acid.
example.chinese: 甘氨酸是最簡單的氨基酸。

corrections.english: The style in which words and phrases are used in writing or speech.
corrections.reason: Phraseology concerns wording and expression style, not syllables.

example.english: The contract uses formal legal phraseology.
example.chinese: 該合約採用正式的法律措辭。

corrections.english: A type or way of weaving.
corrections.reason: This noun denotes a method or pattern of weaving, not armour.

example.english: The fabric has a tight weave.
example.chinese: 這種布料採用緊密的織法。

corrections.english: (electronics) A switch for a jack plug, a jackknife switch; (more generally) a socket used to connect a device to a circuit, network etc. [from 19th c.]
corrections.reason: The electronics sense is a connection socket, not mother.

example.english: Insert the cable into the audio jack.
example.chinese: 把電線插入音訊插孔。

corrections.english: A longitudinal structural member bridging two or more rafters of a roof.
corrections.reason: The architectural member was confused with a malfunction.

example.english: The roof panels were fixed to the purlins.
example.chinese: 屋面板固定在檁條上。

corrections.english: The act of dislocating, or putting out of joint; also, the condition of being thus displaced.
corrections.reason: This anatomical sense is a joint dislocation, not general displacement.

example.english: The fall caused a shoulder dislocation.
example.chinese: 這次跌倒造成肩關節脫臼。

corrections.english: (music, countable) A specific method of using the fingers to play an instrument.
corrections.reason: The musical sense denotes instrument fingering, not typing.

example.english: The teacher corrected my fingering on the piano.
example.chinese: 老師糾正了我的鋼琴指法。

corrections.english: A comic strip.
corrections.reason: The exact noun is a sequential comic strip, not comics in general.

example.english: The newspaper publishes a comic strip every day.
example.chinese: 這份報紙每天刊登一則連環漫畫。

corrections.english: An induced abortion.
corrections.reason: The definition explicitly specifies an induced abortion, not spontaneous miscarriage.

example.english: The law regulates access to induced abortion.
example.chinese: 該法律規管人工流產服務。

corrections.english: Nasal mucus; snot.
corrections.reason: The source uses an obscure mismatched character instead of the ordinary anatomical term.

example.english: He wiped the snivel from the child's nose.
example.chinese: 他擦去孩子鼻子的鼻涕。

corrections.english: Syracuse University (in New York)
corrections.reason: The exact entry denotes the university, not the city of Syracuse.

example.english: She studied journalism at Syracuse University.
example.chinese: 她在雪城大學修讀新聞學。

corrections.english: A castrated ram.
corrections.reason: The definition specifically denotes a castrated ram, not any ram.

example.english: The wether grazed with the flock.
example.chinese: 那隻閹公羊跟羊群一起吃草。

corrections.english: Bad luck.
corrections.reason: This abstract sense means bad luck, not a specific accident.

example.english: They suffered a series of misfortunes.
example.chinese: 他們接連遭遇厄運。

corrections.english: (anatomy) Of, relating to, or being the thyroid gland.
corrections.reason: The source gives the gland noun rather than the adjective.

example.english: The doctor ordered a thyroid test.
example.chinese: 醫生安排了甲狀腺的相關檢查。

corrections.english: (historical) Former name of the Democratic Republic of the Congo: a country in Central Africa; used from 1971–1997.
corrections.reason: The historical country name is conventionally rendered 扎伊爾, while the source form is incomplete.

example.english: The country was known as Zaire until 1997.
example.chinese: 該國在1997年以前稱為扎伊爾。

corrections.english: To accuse of wrongdoing; charge.
corrections.reason: The legal verb means formally accusing or charging someone, not acknowledging receipt.

example.english: The grand jury indicted him on two charges.
example.chinese: 大陪審團以兩項罪名起訴他。

corrections.english: An ancestor.
corrections.reason: The source character alone is not an adequate noun for an ancestor.

example.english: The custom was passed down by our forefathers.
example.chinese: 這項習俗由我們的祖先流傳下來。

corrections.english: The science of determining the order in which events occurred.
corrections.reason: This sense is the discipline of determining event order, not a chronological table.

example.english: Chronology helps historians date ancient events.
example.chinese: 年代學協助歷史學家確定古代事件的日期。

corrections.english: Free from; devoid of; without.
corrections.reason: The source value reverses the meaning of the suffix.

example.english: The café offers sugar-free drinks.
example.chinese: 這家咖啡店提供不含糖的飲品。

corrections.english: A mountain range on the border of France and Switzerland, extending into Germany; in full, Jura Mountains.
corrections.reason: The geographical entry denotes the Jura mountain range, not the Jurassic period.

example.english: The Jura Mountains extend along the French-Swiss border.
example.chinese: 汝拉山脈沿法國與瑞士邊境延伸。

corrections.english: An island between the Taiwan Strait and Philippine Sea in East Asia. [1600s]
corrections.reason: The exact geographical sense is the island of Taiwan, not a state name.

example.english: Taiwan lies in East Asia.
example.chinese: 臺灣位於東亞。

corrections.english: (transitive) To bring into being; give rise to.
corrections.reason: The verb means causing something to exist, not handling or arranging it.

example.english: The panels generate electricity.
example.chinese: 這些面板產生電力。

corrections.english: (specifically) A social environment or setting.
corrections.reason: The source gives only a locative character and misses the social-environment sense.

example.english: The novel portrays an urban artistic milieu.
example.chinese: 這部小說描寫城市藝術家的社會環境。

corrections.english: Made of elastic.
corrections.reason: The source gives the material noun elastic band instead of an adjective.

example.english: The trousers have an elastic waistband.
example.chinese: 這條褲有彈性的腰帶。

corrections.english: (astronomy) A moon of Saturn.
corrections.reason: This astronomy entry denotes Enceladus, Saturn’s moon; the existing Chinese name uses the wrong ordinal form.

example.english: Enceladus is one of Saturn’s moons.
example.chinese: 土衛二是土星的衛星之一。

corrections.english: (astronomy) A moon in Jupiter.
corrections.reason: This astronomy entry denotes Europa, a moon of Jupiter, not the broad transliteration Europa.

example.english: Europa is also called Jupiter II.
example.chinese: 木衛二也稱為 Jupiter II。

corrections.english: The capital city of Ontario, Canada and largest city Canada.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional Chinese for the Canadian city name.

example.english: Toronto is the largest city in Canada.
example.chinese: 多倫多是加拿大最大的城市。

corrections.english: The gases surrounding the Earth or any astronomical body.
corrections.reason: The exact sense is the layer of gases surrounding a planet, not merely air in general.

example.english: The atmosphere protects Earth from some radiation.
example.chinese: 大氣層保護地球免受部分輻射。

corrections.english: A space to be filled in on a form or template.
corrections.reason: The noun means an empty space to be filled in, not the colour white.

example.english: Write your name in the blank.
example.chinese: 請在空格填上你的名字。

corrections.english: A young male horse.
corrections.reason: The entry is a young male horse; “小馬” misses the male-specific sense.

example.english: The colt ran beside its mother.
example.chinese: 那匹小公馬在母馬身旁奔跑。

corrections.english: (informal) An invitation.
corrections.reason: The informal noun means an invitation, not the verb “to invite”.

example.english: I received an invite to the party.
example.chinese: 我收到派對的邀請。

corrections.english: An island of Greece, in the northeastern Aegean Sea.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional Chinese for the Greek island name.

example.english: Lesbos is an island in the Aegean Sea.
example.chinese: 萊斯沃斯島位於愛琴海。

corrections.english: A Pacific island shared by Brunei, Indonesia, and Malaysia; the world’s third largest island.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional Chinese for the island name.

example.english: Borneo is shared by three countries.
example.chinese: 婆羅洲由三個國家分治。

corrections.english: A province and capital city of Indonesia.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional Chinese for the Indonesian capital name.

example.english: Jakarta is the capital of Indonesia.
example.chinese: 雅加達是印尼首都。

corrections.english: (transitive) To use up.
corrections.reason: The verb means to use something up.

example.english: The engine consumes a lot of fuel.
example.chinese: 這具引擎消耗很多燃料。

corrections.english: A heavy rain.
corrections.reason: A downpour is heavy rain, not just a shower.

example.english: The downpour flooded the road.
example.chinese: 那場傾盆大雨淹沒了道路。

corrections.english: A en given name.
corrections.reason: The given-name entry should be the personal name Eve, not a different transliteration.

example.english: Eve introduced herself to the group.
example.chinese: 伊芙向大家自我介紹。

corrections.english: (interrogative) For what cause, reason, or purpose.
corrections.reason: The adverb asks for a cause or reason; “什麼” is too broad and simplified.

example.english: Why did you leave early?
example.chinese: 你為什麼提早離開？

corrections.english: the official and administrative capital of Ivory Coast.
corrections.reason: Use the standard Traditional Chinese form for the capital of Ivory Coast.

example.english: Yamoussoukro is the official capital of Ivory Coast.
example.chinese: 雅穆索戈是象牙海岸的官方首都。

corrections.english: A young female horse.
corrections.reason: The entry is a young female horse, not merely a young horse.

example.english: The filly won her first race.
example.chinese: 那匹小母馬贏得首次賽事。

corrections.english: Relating to the Basque people or their language.
corrections.reason: The adjective means relating to the Basque people or language, not the language noun itself.

example.english: Basque culture has a long history.
example.chinese: 巴斯克的文化有悠久歷史。

corrections.english: Causing or tending to cause cancer.
corrections.reason: The adjective means causing cancer, not a carcinogenic substance.

example.english: Smoking releases carcinogenic chemicals.
example.chinese: 吸煙會釋放致癌的化學物質。

corrections.english: The day immediately before today; one day ago.
corrections.reason: The day immediately before today is yesterday, not the day before yesterday.

example.english: Yesterday was colder than today.
example.chinese: 昨天比今天冷。

corrections.english: The combination of circumstances at a given moment; a state of affairs.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional Chinese and preserve the state-of-affairs sense.

example.english: The situation changed quickly.
example.chinese: 情況很快改變了。

corrections.english: (phonology) The final part of a syllable, the combination of medial and rime in phonetics and phonology.
corrections.reason: In phonology, final means the final part of a syllable, i.e. a rime/final, not “last” in general.

example.english: The syllable final affects the pronunciation.
example.chinese: 韻尾會影響發音。

corrections.english: A person's self: general form of himself, herself, themself or yourself.
corrections.reason: The pronoun means a person’s self, not an individual person in general.

example.english: One should be honest with oneself.
example.chinese: 一個人應該對自己誠實。

corrections.english: (grammar) Relating to an auxiliary verb.
corrections.reason: The adjective means relating to an auxiliary verb; the current noun phrase gives the verb category instead.

example.english: This sentence contains an auxiliary verb.
example.chinese: 這個句子含有輔助的動詞成分。

corrections.english: An island and overseas territory of the United Kingdom in the Caribbean. Official name: Montserrat.
corrections.reason: Use the standard Traditional Chinese name of the Caribbean territory.

example.english: Montserrat is a British overseas territory.
example.chinese: 蒙特塞拉特是英國海外領土。

corrections.english: A en given name.
corrections.reason: Use the common Traditional Chinese transliteration of the given name Kevin.

example.english: Kevin opened the meeting.
example.chinese: 凱文主持了會議。

corrections.english: To make easy or easier.
corrections.reason: The verb means to make something easier, not merely “convenient” as an adjective.

example.english: The new system will facilitate communication.
example.chinese: 新系統會使便利跨部門溝通。

corrections.english: (most often) Short for clothes dryer, tumble dryer
corrections.reason: Use Traditional Chinese for clothes dryer.

example.english: The dryer stopped working.
example.chinese: 烘乾機停止運作。

corrections.english: A committee that manages the business of an organization, e.g., a board of directors.
corrections.reason: The noun denotes a managing committee such as a board of directors.

example.english: The board approved the plan.
example.chinese: 董事會批准了計劃。

corrections.english: A en given name.
corrections.reason: The given-name entry should be the personal name Ryan, not “little king”.

example.english: Ryan joined the team last year.
example.chinese: 萊恩去年加入了團隊。

corrections.english: A glass of sherry.
corrections.reason: The sense is a glass of sherry, not a cup in general.

example.english: She ordered a sherry before dinner.
example.chinese: 她晚餐前點了一杯雪利酒。

corrections.english: A light, sweet, white wine from the Canary Islands.
corrections.reason: This sense denotes a type of white wine from the Canary Islands, not the bird.

example.english: The menu listed a sweet Canary wine.
example.chinese: 餐牌列出一款加那利白葡萄酒。

corrections.english: A en given name.
corrections.reason: Use the common Traditional Chinese transliteration of the given name Frank.

example.english: Frank called this morning.
example.chinese: 弗蘭克今早來電。

corrections.english: A en given name.
corrections.reason: The given-name entry should be Naomi, not an unrelated common noun.

example.english: Naomi works in the same office.
example.chinese: 娜奧米在同一間辦公室工作。

corrections.english: A en given name.
corrections.reason: Use the common Traditional Chinese transliteration of the given name Peter.

example.english: Peter answered the question.
example.chinese: 彼得回答了問題。

corrections.english: Early; premature.
corrections.reason: Untimely means premature or ill-timed, not the colour green.

example.english: His untimely death shocked everyone.
example.chinese: 他過早的死亡震驚眾人。

corrections.english: Having already happened; in the past; finished. [from 14th c.]
corrections.reason: The adjective means having already happened, not only the noun “past”.

example.english: Past mistakes can teach us lessons.
example.chinese: 過去的錯誤可以給我們教訓。

corrections.english: A en given name.
corrections.reason: The given-name entry should be Esther, not the Book of Esther.

example.english: Esther arrived early.
example.chinese: 以斯帖很早到達。

corrections.english: To act as an understudy (to someone).
corrections.reason: The verb means to act as an understudy, not simply to replace.

example.english: She understudied the lead role.
example.chinese: 她在那齣劇中當替角。

corrections.english: (also loaf of bread) A block of bread after baking.
corrections.reason: A loaf is a block of bread after baking.

example.english: He bought a loaf at the bakery.

corrections.english: (transitive) To make available to the public.
corrections.reason: The verb means to make something available to the public.

corrections.reason: To join means to become a member of a group; 入伙 is too colloquial/narrow.

example.english: She joined the club.
example.chinese: 她加入了俱樂部。

corrections.english: A light meal.
corrections.reason: A snack is a light meal or small amount of food between meals.

corrections.english: Joy; pleasure.
corrections.reason: Delight means joy or pleasure.

corrections.english: to give birth to or produce
corrections.reason: To bear means to give birth to or produce; keep both birth and produce/fruit senses.

example.english: The tree bears fruit in summer.
example.chinese: 這棵樹在夏天結出果實。

corrections.english: to write or include items in a list
corrections.reason: To list means to write or include items in a list.

example.english: The guide lists several local restaurants.
example.chinese: 指南列出了幾間本地餐廳。

corrections.english: a business or company
corrections.reason: Firm in this noun sense means a business or company; keep the existing natural translation.

corrections.english: a poker hand in which all the cards are of the same suit
corrections.reason: A flush is a poker hand in which all cards are of the same suit.

example.english: She won the game with a flush.
example.chinese: 她以同花贏了這局牌。

corrections.english: Clothing.
corrections.reason: Apparel means clothing; 穿戴 is not the best noun gloss.

corrections.english: An error.
corrections.reason: Mistake means an error; keep the clear modern gloss.

example.english: You made the same mistake as last time.
example.chinese: 你犯了跟上次相同的錯誤。

corrections.english: (anatomy) Either corner of the eye, where the eyelids meet.
corrections.reason: Canthus is either corner of the eye.

corrections.english: to help someone
corrections.reason: To aid means to help someone.

example.english: The money will aid poor families.
example.chinese: 這筆錢會援助貧困家庭。

corrections.english: loyal and faithful
corrections.reason: True in the loyal sense means loyal and faithful.

corrections.english: to shoot a gun or weapon
corrections.reason: To fire a weapon means to shoot or discharge it.

example.english: The soldiers fired at the target.
example.chinese: 士兵向目標開火。

corrections.english: to have snow falling from the sky
corrections.reason: To snow means to have snow falling from the sky; 漫天飛舞 is too poetic.

example.english: It snowed all night.
example.chinese: 整晚都在下雪。

corrections.english: to discuss something
corrections.reason: To talk about something means to discuss it.

example.english: They talked about the plan.
example.chinese: 他們討論了這個計畫。

corrections.english: Foam.
corrections.reason: Froth means foam.

corrections.english: cloth used for making clothes or other items
corrections.reason: Material in this sense is cloth used for making clothes or other items.

example.english: We chose a soft material for the curtains.
example.chinese: 我們為窗簾選了一種柔軟布料。

corrections.english: to put something in a particular position
corrections.reason: To place means to put something in a particular position.

corrections.english: to help someone do something
corrections.reason: To assist means to help someone do something.

example.english: A member of staff will assist you.
example.chinese: 一名職員會協助你。

corrections.english: At this moment, at present, now.
corrections.reason: Currently means at this moment, at present, now.

corrections.english: to receive, obtain, or buy something
corrections.reason: To get means to receive, obtain, or buy something.

example.english: I need to get a new phone.
example.chinese: 我需要得到一部新手機。

corrections.english: (by extension) The world of commerce and trade.
corrections.reason: Marketplace by extension means the world of commerce and trade.

corrections.english: physical strength or power
corrections.reason: Force in this noun sense means physical strength or power.

example.english: The door opened with force.
example.chinese: 門被用力量推開。

corrections.english: to go or come back, or give something back
corrections.reason: To return means to go or come back, or give something back.

example.english: Please return the book tomorrow.
example.chinese: 請明天歸還這本書。

corrections.english: to make an effort to do something
corrections.reason: To try means to make an effort to do something.

example.english: Try to open the door.
example.chinese: 嘗試打開門。

corrections.english: (transitive) To butcher animals, generally for food.
corrections.reason: To slaughter animals means to butcher them for food.

corrections.english: making little or no noise
corrections.reason: Quiet means making little or no noise; reject the noisy 悄悄 candidate.

corrections.english: To have respect for.
corrections.reason: To respect means to have respect for.

corrections.english: to fall as rain
corrections.reason: To rain means to fall as rain.

example.english: It may rain tonight.
example.chinese: 今晚可能會下雨。

corrections.english: A strip of fabric worn around the head.
corrections.reason: A headband is a strip of fabric worn around the head.

corrections.english: At the present time.
corrections.reason: Now means at the present time.

corrections.english: On the day before today.
corrections.reason: Yesterday means on the day before today.

corrections.english: frozen water
corrections.reason: Ice is frozen water.

example.english: The lake was covered with ice.
example.chinese: 湖面覆蓋著冰。
